source_jobs.title: Mid-Level Full Stack Engineer
source_jobs.description:
COMPANY OVERVIEW

Every year, one million businesses shut down, yet the process remains messy, expensive, and underserved. At SimpleClosure, our mission is to make business shutdowns seamless, compliant, and stress free, because closing a company shouldn’t feel harder than starting one.

We recently raised our $15 million Series A and were named one of Fast Company’s Most Innovative Companies of 2025. Backed by top investors and trusted by hundreds of startups, we’re rapidly establishing ourselves as the technology leader in this space. Join us and make an outsized impact as we create innovative solutions for businesses, their teams, and their stakeholders.


JOB OVERVIEW

SimpleClosure is seeking a Mid-Level Full Stack Engineer to join our growing engineering team. In this role, you’ll work across the stack to build, improve, and scale core product functionality that directly impacts our customers.

You’ll collaborate closely with product, design, and business stakeholders to ship reliable, user-centric features while taking ownership of meaningful parts of the codebase. This role is ideal for an engineer who excels in frontend craftsmanship and likes to delve into backend problem-solving in a fast-moving startup environment.

This position reports to the CTO.]

*Ideal candidates MUST be located in the New York City Metro area.


RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Design, build, and maintain full-stack features across the SimpleClosure platform

 - Collaborate with Product and Design to translate requirements into scalable technical solutions

 - Implement clean, maintainable frontend experiences using React and TypeScript

 - Build and integrate backend services, APIs, and data models to support product workflows

 - Write high-quality, well-tested code and participate in code reviews

 - Own features end-to-end, from technical design through deployment and iteration

 - Ensure performance, reliability, accessibility, and responsiveness across devices

 - Debug, troubleshoot, and resolve issues in production environments

 - Continuously improve development processes, tooling, and code quality


DESIRED SKILLS AND QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience: 3–5 years of professional software engineering experience, ideally in a startup or fast-paced environment

 - Frontend: Strong experience with React and TypeScript; Next.js or similar frameworks preferred

 - Backend: Experience implementing APIs and connecting with third-party services

 - Databases: Familiarity with relational and/or NoSQL databases

 - Ownership: Comfortable taking projects from concept through deployment and iteration

 - Problem-Solving: Proactive, thoughtful engineer who can navigate ambiguity and make sound technical decisions

 - Collaboration: Strong team player who enjoys working cross-functionally

 - Communication: Clear written and verbal communication skills

 - Adaptability: Thrives in an evolving startup environment with shifting priorities

 - AI-Native: Familiar with latest agentic and coding technologies, trusts but verifies model outputs


WHAT WE OFFER

 - Compensation: $120,000 - $170,000

 - Unlimited PTO

 - Competitive equity package

 - Employer Covered Medical Benefits

 - Hybrid work in New York City (Midtown) - two days in office, three days remote

 - Two in-person team retreats a year

 - 401(k) retirement plan with Traditional & Roth options and immediate eligibility

*We are unable to support any visa outside of Canada (no H1B Visas)

source_jobs.title: Commercial Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
COMPANY OVERVIEW

Every year, one million businesses shut down, yet the process remains messy, expensive, and underserved. At SimpleClosure, our mission is to make business shutdowns seamless, compliant, and stress free, because closing a company shouldn’t feel harder than starting one.

We recently raised our $15 million Series A and were named one of Fast Company’s Most Innovative Companies of 2025. Backed by top investors and trusted by hundreds of startups, we’re rapidly establishing ourselves as the technology leader in this space. Join us and make an outsized impact as we create innovative solutions for businesses, their teams, and their stakeholders.


JOB OVERVIEW

We are seeking a driven and dynamic Commercial Account Executive to support our sales efforts, engage with potential clients, and help grow our customer base. The ideal candidate has a passion for sales, strong communication skills, and a desire to work in a fast-paced startup environment.


RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Assist in identifying, researching, and qualifying prospective customers navigating business closures

 - Support the sales team in managing the sales pipeline and tracking key metrics.

 - Provide product demonstrations and educate potential clients on our solutions.

 - Support deal progression by managing follow-ups, next steps, and customer questions

 - Collaborate with marketing to optimize lead generation and conversion strategies.

 - Maintain accurate records of sales activities in CRM software (e.g., HubSpot, Salesforce).

 - Gather and report customer feedback to help refine sales strategies and product development.

 - Stay up-to-date with industry trends, competitor activity, and client needs.


DESIRED SKILLS AND QUALIFICATIONS

 - Legal background, understanding of M&A, corporate law, or accounting experience (preferred but not necessary)

 - 2 - 4+ years sales experience in SaaS/Startup environment; in Legal-tech, Fintech, HR-tech.

 - Strong communication and interpersonal skills with a customer-first mindset.

 - Ability to work independently, stay organized, and manage multiple priorities.

 - Comfortable conducting outreach and engaging with potential clients.

 - Experience with CRM tools like HubSpot or Salesforce (preferred but not required).

 - Enthusiastic, goal-oriented, and eager to learn in a startup environment.

 - Bachelor’s degree in business, marketing, or a related field.


WHAT WE OFFER

 - Compensation: This role is fully remote and open to candidates across the United States. OTE is determined by location tier:
   
   - Tier 1 (NYC, LA, SF): $170,000 – $180,000 OTE
   
   - Tier 2 (Chicago, Austin, Denver, Boston, DC, Miami): $160,000 – $170,000 OTE
   
   - Tier 3 (All other US locations): $150,000 – $160,000 OTE

 - Unlimited PTO

 - Competitive equity package

 - Employer Covered Medical Benefits

 - Fully remote

 - Two in-person team retreats a year

 - 401(k) retirement plan with Traditional & Roth options and immediate eligibility

*We are unable to support any visa outside of Canada (no H1B Visas)

source_jobs.title: Strategy & Operations - Asset Hub
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE COMPANY

Every year, one million businesses shut down, yet the process remains messy, expensive, and underserved. At SimpleClosure, our mission is to make business shutdowns seamless, compliant, and stress free, because closing a company shouldn’t feel harder than starting one.

We recently raised our $15 million Series A and were named one of Fast Company’s Most Innovative Companies of 2025. Backed by top investors and trusted by hundreds of startups, we’re rapidly establishing ourselves as the technology leader in this space. Join us and make an outsized impact as we create innovative solutions for businesses, their teams, and their stakeholders.


JOB OVERVIEW

We're looking for a dynamic and results-driven Strategy & Operations to join our AssetHub team. AssetHub helps our customers sell proprietary assets to buyers; returning value to founders, investors, and lenders through unique partnerships with leading AI companies and other buyers. We're experiencing fast growth and have the potential to build a massive new marketplace, making this a unique opportunity to join early and shape a product with real impact.

You'll own the full operational lifecycle of our supply side (asset sellers), from initial outreach through deal close. You'll track where customers are in their journey, ensure nothing falls through the cracks, and build and optimize the systems that make it all run and scale. We use AI extensively (including Claude) to automate where possible, and we want someone who leans into that, not away from it.

This role is both internal and external facing. We're looking for someone that puts customers first and is feels warm inside when their holistic experience with us is amazing. It is not a typical CSM role, however - our customers are founders of small and medium-sized businesses, and our engagement with them is short. Success here is about earning trust quickly, guiding founders through an unfamiliar process with clarity and empathy, and maximizing the value returned to everyone involved, with as little friction for the founder as possible.

*Ideal candidates MUST be located in the New York City Metro area.


RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Maximize asset supply — Proactively work to maximize the number of high-quality assets listed in our catalog. This means constantly evaluating and optimizing our customer funnel with product and ops teams, to convert the most leads into active asset sellers.

 - Own the customer lifecycle end-to-end — Track and manage customer progress from first contact through deal execution. Identify when customers are stuck and unblock them with minimal effort required on their end.

 - Guide founders through the process — Be the empathetic, knowledgeable point of contact for founders considering or actively completing an asset sale. Understand their concerns, answer their questions, and communicate clearly throughout.

 - Build and automate workflows — Identify repetitive or manual steps in the process and build systems (with AI tools like Claude and platforms like HubSpot) to automate them, improving both throughput and customer experience.

 - Drive cross-functional initiatives — Collaborate with marketing, sales, engineering, and product to build scalable growth loops and improve the seller experience.

 - Launch and grow new offerings — Act as both thought partner and operator, with the autonomy to conceptualize and build new services within AssetHub.


DESIRED SKILLS AND QUALIFICATIONS

 - 5+ years of experience in sales/CS/operations in SaaS/Startup environment (or an MBA and 3+ years of the same experience)

 - You are commercially minded. You understand what moves a deal forward. You know how to build trust with founders and you know how to close.

 - You are results oriented and enjoy seeing a customer happy with your service.

 - You are independent and proactive, and also comfortable being scrappy and adapting as we move fast.

 - You have a track record of driving projects across

source_jobs.title: VC/Accelerator Partnerships
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE COMPANY

Every year, one million businesses shut down, yet the process remains messy, expensive, and underserved. At SimpleClosure, our mission is to make business shutdowns seamless, compliant, and stress free, because closing a company shouldn’t feel harder than starting one.

We recently raised our $15 million Series A and were named one of Fast Company’s Most Innovative Companies of 2025. Backed by top investors and trusted by hundreds of startups, we’re rapidly establishing ourselves as the technology leader in this space. Join us and make an outsized impact as we create innovative solutions for businesses, their teams, and their stakeholders.


JOB OVERVIEW

This isn't a sales role.

It's a relationships role — built on genuine trust inside one of the world's most connected startup ecosystems. You will own SimpleClosure's presence inside the SF VC and accelerator community: the platform teams, the program managers, the partners who quietly shepherd portfolio companies through hard transitions.

You'll be an integral part of our Partnerships team, supporting the SF market end-to-end: sourcing new partners, deepening existing ones, co-designing events, and building the kind of earned credibility that no outbound sequence can manufacture.

*Ideal candidates MUST be located in the San Francisco Metro Area.


RESPONSIBILITIES

Partnerships & Pipeline

 - Own a portfolio of VC fund and accelerator relationships from first outreach through activation and long-term engagement

 - Map the SF VC landscape: identify funds with the most portfolio distress exposure, surface platform leads and program managers, and prioritize by referral potential

 - Build and maintain referral pipelines with VC fund stakeholders, platform teams, accelerator program managers, startup attorneys, and ecosystem connectors

 - Track all relationship activity, referral volume, and pipeline contribution in HubSpot — this is a real pipeline, and it needs to look like one

 - Own the handoff loop: when a partner sends us a company, they should hear back fast and feel good about it

Ecosystem Presence

 - Attend 3–5 Bay Area startup events per week — demo days, VC-hosted dinners, founder meetups, tech week gatherings, legal and accounting firm events

 - Build genuine relationships with: VC GPs/partners, finance and legal teams, and platform leads (especially those managing portfolio companies navigating wind-downs), accelerator program managers (YC, a16z, Soma Capital, First Round, etc.) and directly with founders.

 - Become the recognized SimpleClosure face in SF — the name that surfaces when the words "shutdown" or "wind-down" come up in conversation

Events & Programming

 - Support and co-own SimpleClosure's SF event programming alongside our Community team: founder dinners, VC roundtables, informal "what to do when it's over" conversations

 - Identify co-hosting and co-branding opportunities with ecosystem partners that extend reach without requiring a full production lift

 - Bring back intel from every room you're in: what are founders worried about, what are VCs telling their portfolio, what language resonates


DESIRED SKILLS AND QUALIFICATIONS

 - Already embedded in the SF VC ecosystem — you have existing relationships at VC platform teams, accelerator programs, or startup law firms

 - 3–5+ years in partnerships, BD, ecosystem, platform or community roles at a startup, financial services or VC

 - Organized enough to run a real pipeline in CRM; not so transactional that you lose the trust that makes this work

 - Genuine social energy — you are energized by events and people, not depleted by them. This is a personality fit, not a hustle requirement.

 - Strong communicator: warm in person, crisp in writing, responsive in Slack

 - Comfortable with ambiguity and self-directed; you don't need a playbook to get moving

Desired but not required:

 - Personal experience founding or shutting down a company — you know what this moment feels

notes: Series A startup with a mix of tech and non-tech roles
